# Data Dictionary - WHO SMART Guidelines - HIV v0.4.4

* [**Table of Contents**](toc.md)
* [**Business Requirements**](business-requirements.md)
* **Data Dictionary**

## Data Dictionary

Component 5 in the Digital Adaptation Kit (DAK) for HIV (link forthcoming) outlines the minimum set of data corresponding to different points of the workflow within the identified business processes. This data set can be used on any softwaresystem and lists the data elements relevant for service delivery and executing decision-support logic, as well as for populating indicators and performance metrics.

See Web Annex A of the DAK for the complete data dictionary in spreadsheet form detailing the input options, validation checks and concept dictionary codes.

## HIV.A Registration Data

* Activity ID: HIV.A2 Gather client details
  * Data Element ID: HIV.A.DE1
  * Data Element Label: First name
  * Description and Definition: Client's first or given name
  * Multiple Choice Type (if applicable): N/A
  * Data Type: String
  * Input Options: N/A
  * Quantity Sub-type: N/A
  * Calculation: N/A
  * Validation Condition: Only letters and special characters (period, dash) allowed
  * Required: R
  * Explain Conditionality: None
  * Linkages to Decision Support Tables: 
  * Linkages to Aggregate Indicators: 
  * Annotations: 
* Activity ID: HIV.A2 Gather client details
  * Data Element ID: HIV.A.DE2
  * Data Element Label: Family name
  * Description and Definition: Client's family name or last name
  * Multiple Choice Type (if applicable): N/A
  * Data Type: String
  * Input Options: N/A
  * Quantity Sub-type: N/A
  * Calculation: N/A
  * Validation Condition: Only letters and special characters (period, dash) allowed
  * Required: R
  * Explain Conditionality: None
  * Linkages to Decision Support Tables: 
  * Linkages to Aggregate Indicators: 
  * Annotations: 
* Activity ID: HIV.A2 Gather client details
  * Data Element ID: HIV.A.DE3
  * Data Element Label: Visit date
  * Description and Definition: The date and time of the client's visit
  * Multiple Choice Type (if applicable): N/A
  * Data Type: DateTime
  * Input Options: N/A
  * Quantity Sub-type: N/A
  * Calculation: N/A
  * Validation Condition: DateTime ≤ Current DateTime
  * Required: R
  * Explain Conditionality: None
  * Linkages to Decision Support Tables: HIV.B7.DT, HIV.B9.DT, HIV.D17.DT, HIV.E4.DT, HIV.S.1, HIV.S.2, HIV.S.3, HIV.S.4
  * Linkages to Aggregate Indicators: HEP.1B, HEP.2B, STI.1B, STI.4B, STI.7B, STI.8B
  * Annotations: The encounter date may be system generated, but there is a requirement to be able to back enter data.
* Activity ID: HIV.A2 Gather client details

  * Data Element ID: HIV.A.DE17

Other data elements may be used in the calculation of this data element (e.g. box 2.4 from the same chapter). Note that this data element should only be used to include people for service provision and not exclude people.

  * Data Element ID: HIV.C.DE57
  * Data Element Label: On ART less than 6 months
  * Description and Definition: Sex partner is HIV-positive and is on ART less than 6 months. ART may take up to 6 months to suppress viral load. In studies of serodiscordant couples, pre-exposure prophylaxis (PrEP) has provided a useful bridge to full viral suppression by the partner during that time.

  * Description and Definition: Value used for gender for calculating creatinine clearance if required. For transgender populations, the sex at birth is used in the Cockcroft-Gault equation if the person is not using hormone therapy; among transgender populations using hormone therapy for more than three months, the current gender can be used.
  * Multiple Choice Type (if applicable): N/A

* Activity ID: HIV.C23 Prescribe or administer PrEP or PEP
  * Data Element ID: HIV.C.DE110
  * Data Element Label: Estimated creatinine clearance (Cockcroft–Gault equation)
  * Description and Definition: If the laboratory does not have the capacity to estimate creatinine clearance, the provider can use the Cockcroft–Gault equation to calculate estimated creatinine clearance based on measured serum creatinine, the client’s sex at birth, age and estimated lean body weight.
  * Multiple Choice Type (if applicable): N/A
  * Data Type: Quantity
  * Input Options: N/A
  * Quantity Sub-type: Integer Quantity
  * Calculation: “Sex factor” * ((140 – Age) / (“Serum creatinine test result”)) * (Weight / 72) Sex factor: male = 1, female = 0.85 Age in years, serum creatinine in mg/DL, weight in kg
  * Validation Condition: None
  * Required: O
  * Explain Conditionality: None
  * Linkages to Decision Support Tables: 
  * Linkages to Aggregate Indicators: 
  * Annotations:

* Activity ID: HIV.D8 Capture or update client history
  * Data Element ID: HIV.D.DE53
  * Data Element Label: Date enrolled in HIV care
  * Description and Definition: Date client first enrols in HIV care at the facility. Begins when a person with a confirmed HIV diagnosis presents to a facility where HIV care is provided and a medical record, patient card, file or chart is opened for the first time. This could be at an HIV care/ART, MNCH or TB clinic.
  * Multiple Choice Type (if applicable): N/A
  * Data Type: Date
  * Input Options: N/A
  * Quantity Sub-type: N/A
  * Calculation: N/A
  * Validation Condition: None
  * Required: O
  * Explain Conditionality: None
  * Linkages to Decision Support Tables: 
  * Linkages to Aggregate Indicators: 
  * Annotations: WHO recommends that all patients be enrolled in HIV care at their first facility visit following an HIV-positive diagnosis (which may take place in the same facility or on the same day as the HIV diagnosis). For patients who may have received prior ART, "newly enrolled" includes treatment-experienced patients with or without clinical records who received ART from sources outside the system (e.g. patient seen by private practitioner, patient buys drugs themselves or is sent drugs), or PrEP or short-course ARV prophylaxis for PMTCT, and have not been counted as "newly enrolled" in a system that is being monitored nationally. If a facility receives a treatment-experienced patient without records who was previously treated at a facility that reports to the national programme (and, therefore, reported as "newly enrolled" once before), an attempt should be made to retrieve the records and confirm that the patient was previously on treatment.
* Activity ID: HIV.D8 Capture or update client history

* Activity ID: HIV.D8 Capture or update client history
  * Data Element ID: HIV.D.DE89
  * Data Element Label: Third-line ART regimen
  * Description and Definition: Third-line ART regimen for people living with HIV (Not defined by WHO. National programmes should develop policies for third-line ART)
  * Multiple Choice Type (if applicable): Input Option
  * Data Type: Codes
  * Input Options: Third-line ART regimen
  * Quantity Sub-type: N/A
  * Calculation: N/A
  * Validation Condition: None
  * Required: O
  * Explain Conditionality: None
  * Linkages to Decision Support Tables: HIV.D21.1.DT, HIV.D21.2.DT
  * Linkages to Aggregate Indicators: 
  * Annotations: ART national programmes should develop policies for third-line ART (conditional recommendation, low-quality evidence). Third-line regimens should include new drugs with minimal risk of cross-resistance to previously used regimens, such as INSTIs and second-generation NNRTIs and PIs (conditional recommendation, low-quality evidence). Patients on a failing second-line regimen with no new ARV options should continue with a tolerated regimen (conditional recommendation, very low-quality evidence). Consolidated guidelines on the use of antiretroviral drugs for treating and preventing HIV infection: recommendations for a public health approach. Geneva: World Health Organization; 2013 (http://www.who. int/hiv/pub/guidelines/arv2013/download/en).

* Activity ID: HIV.D17 Check for signs of treatment failure
  * Data Element ID: HIV.D.DE228
  * Data Element Label: Virological failure
  * Description and Definition: The inability to achieve or maintain viral suppression below a certain threshold indicated by viral load above 1000 copies/mL based on two consecutive viral load measurements in 3 months, with adherence support following the first viral load test
  * Multiple Choice Type (if applicable): Input Option
  * Data Type: Codes
  * Input Options: Virological failure
  * Quantity Sub-type: N/A
  * Calculation: N/A
  * Validation Condition: None
  * Required: O

  * Linkages to Decision Support Tables: HIV.D17.DT
  * Linkages to Aggregate Indicators: 
  * Annotations: Description may be updated to be generic as criteria is defined in the decision logic. However, as it is part of the WHO guideline defining how the failure is defined, it is included with the data element.

* Activity ID: HIV.D15 Determine clinical stage of HIV
  * Data Element ID: HIV.D.DE306
  * Data Element Label: Unexplained moderate malnutrition not adequately responding to standard therapy
  * Description and Definition: Defined as weight-for-height < - 2 z-score or mid-upper arm circumference 115 mm to <125 mm. For children younger than five years of age: stunting is defined as severe acute malnutrition - either weight for height < -3 z-score or mid-upper arm circumference <115 mm or the presence of oedema.

* Activity ID: HIV.D15 Determine clinical stage of HIV
  * Data Element ID: HIV.D.DE324
  * Data Element Label: HIV wasting syndrome
  * Description and Definition: Unexplained involuntary weight loss (>10% baseline body weight), with obvious wasting or body mass index <18.5; PLUS EITHER unexplained chronic reported for longer than one month; OR reports of fever or night sweats for more than one month without other cause and lack of response to antibiotics or antimalarial agents; malaria must be excluded in malarious areas.
  * Multiple Choice Type (if applicable): Input Option
  * Data Type: Codes
  * Input Options: HIV wasting syndrome
  * Quantity Sub-type: N/A
  * Calculation: N/A
  * Validation Condition: None
  * Required: O
  * Explain Conditionality: None
  * Linkages to Decision Support Tables: HIV.D15.DT, HIV.D17.DT
  * Linkages to Aggregate Indicators: 
  * Annotations:

* Activity ID: HIV.D21 Determine regimen and treatment options
  * Data Element ID: HIV.D.DE466
  * Data Element Label: Type of treatment-limiting toxicity
  * Description and Definition: Type of treatment-limiting toxicity experienced by client. Treatment-limiting toxicity is defined as a serious adverse drug reaction that results in drug discontinuation or substitution. In addition, any reaction that leads to treatment interruption or requires changing the drug or regimen because of an adverse drug reaction is also considered a serious adverse drug reaction.
  * Multiple Choice Type (if applicable): Select all that apply
  * Data Type: Coding
  * Input Options: N/A
  * Quantity Sub-type: N/A
  * Calculation: N/A
  * Validation Condition: None
  * Required: O
  * Explain Conditionality: None
  * Linkages to Decision Support Tables: 
  * Linkages to Aggregate Indicators: ART.9
  * Annotations: Treatment-limiting toxicity is defined as a serious adverse drug reaction that results in drug discontinuation or substitution. In addition, any reaction that leads to treatment interruption or requires changing the drug or regimen because of an adverse drug reaction is also considered a serious adverse drug reaction (See WHO (2022) Consolidated guidelines on person-centred HIV strategic information, web annex H)

  * Data Element ID: HIV.E.DE11
  * Data Element Label: Number of pregnancies (gravida)
  * Description and Definition: Total number of times the woman has been pregnant (including this pregnancy). Also referred to as gravida.

  * Data Type: Quantity
  * Input Options: N/A
  * Quantity Sub-type: Integer Quantity

* Activity ID: HIV.E1 Capture or update mother's history
  * Data Element ID: HIV.E.DE12
  * Data Element Label: Number of previous pregnancies
  * Description and Definition: This calculates the total number of all previous pregnancies (i.e. not including this current pregnancy). This is done for easier obstetric history calculations.
  * Multiple Choice Type (if applicable): N/A
  * Data Type: Quantity
  * Input Options: N/A
  * Quantity Sub-type: Integer Quantity
  * Calculation: "Number of pregnancies (gravida)" - 1

  * Linkages to Aggregate Indicators: 
  * Annotations:

  * Data Element ID: HIV.E.DE13
  * Data Element Label: Number of miscarriages and/or abortions
  * Description and Definition: Total number of pregnancies lost or ended due to miscarriages and/or abortions before 22 weeks / 5 months

  * Data Type: Quantity
  * Input Options: N/A
  * Quantity Sub-type: Integer Quantity
  * Calculation: N/A
  * Validation Condition: 0 ≤ "Number of miscarriages and/or abortions" ≤ "Number of previous pregnancies"

  * Annotations:

  * Data Element Label: Number of live births
  * Description and Definition: Total number of live births after 22 weeks
  * Multiple Choice Type (if applicable): N/A
  * Data Type: Quantity
  * Input Options: N/A
  * Quantity Sub-type: Integer Quantity

  * Validation Condition: 0 ≤ "Number of live births" ≤ ("Number of previous pregnancies" - "Number of miscarriages and/or abortions")

* Activity ID: HIV.E1 Capture or update mother's history
  * Data Element ID: HIV.E.DE20
  * Data Element Label: Pre-eclampsia
  * Description and Definition: A condition specific to pregnancy, arising after the 20th week of gestation, characterized by hypertension and proteinuria. Oedema may also be present, but is no longer considered a cardinal sign because it is present to some extent in most pregnancies. If not controlled, pre-eclampsia will lead to eclampsia which is characterized by fits, followed by coma, and has a high mortality rate.
  * Multiple Choice Type (if applicable): Input Option
  * Data Type: Codes
  * Input Options: Pre-eclampsia
  * Quantity Sub-type: N/A
  * Calculation: N/A
  * Validation Condition: None
  * Required: O
  * Explain Conditionality: None
  * Linkages to Decision Support Tables: 
  * Linkages to Aggregate Indicators: 
  * Annotations: See ANC DAK
* Activity ID: HIV.E1 Capture or update mother's history
  * Data Element ID: HIV.E.DE21
  * Data Element Label: Eclampsia
  * Description and Definition: A condition peculiar to pregnancy or a newly delivered woman, characterized by fits followed coma. The woman usually has hypertension and proteinuria. The fits may occur in the antepartum, intrapartum or early postpartum periods.

  * Data Type: Codes

  * Description and Definition: BP that is less than 140/90 mmHg at a clinic/office visit, but greater then or equal to 135/85 mmHg at other times outside the clinic/ office (pre-pregnancy or at less than 20 weeks)
  * Multiple Choice Type (if applicable): Input Option
  * Data Type: Codes
  * Input Options: Masked hypertension
  * Quantity Sub-type: N/A
  * Calculation: N/A
  * Validation Condition: None
  * Required: O
  * Explain Conditionality: None
  * Linkages to Decision Support Tables: 
  * Linkages to Aggregate Indicators: 
  * Annotations: 
* Activity ID: HIV.E1 Capture or update mother's history
  * Data Element ID: HIV.E.DE270
  * Data Element Label: Gestational hypertension
  * Description and Definition: Hypertension arising de novo at greater than or equal to 20 weeks' gestation in the absence of proteinuria or other findings suggestive of pre-eclampsia
  * Multiple Choice Type (if applicable): Input Option
  * Data Type: Codes
  * Input Options: Gestational hypertension
  * Quantity Sub-type: N/A

  * Data Element ID: HIV.E.DE271
  * Data Element Label: Transient gestational hypertension
  * Description and Definition: Hypertension arising at greater than or equal to 20 weeks' gestation in the clinic, which resolves with repeated BP readings
  * Multiple Choice Type (if applicable): Input Option
  * Data Type: Codes
  * Input Options: Transient gestational hypertension

* Activity ID: HIV.I6 Provide information to referral facility
  * Data Element ID: HIV.I.DE17
  * Data Element Label: Client history summary
  * Description and Definition: With interoperable systems, the provider receiving the referral should be able to access the client's health record digitally. However, in the absence of this, the referral provider should receive a summary of the client's health records that includes the client's history, medications, medications prescribed or dispensed, reported issues and concerns, and any other relevant clinical information the health care provider had already obtained.
  * Multiple Choice Type (if applicable): N/A

## HIV.Prevention Data

* Activity ID: HIV.B6 Capture or update client history
  * Data Element ID: HIV.PRV.DE1
  * Data Element Label: At elevated risk for HIV acquisition
  * Description and Definition: Client is at elevated risk for HIV acquisition, defined according to country/programme context
  * Multiple Choice Type (if applicable): N/A
  * Data Type: Boolean
  * Input Options: True/False
  * Quantity Sub-type: N/A
  * Calculation: This data element be calculated, and updated through time, in a number of ways based upon country or programme context. See chapter 3 (Prevention) of the 2022 WHO Consolidated guidelines on HIV strategic inforamation.
  * Validation Condition: None
  * Required: O
  * Explain Conditionality: None
  * Linkages to Decision Support Tables: HIV.B9.DT, HIV.S.4
  * Linkages to Aggregate Indicators: HTS.7, HTS.8, PRV.3
  * Annotations: Chapter 3 of the SI GL provides an example questionnaire (used at POC) that could be used to calculate this data element. Other data elements may be used in the calculation of this data element (e.g. True if "Signs of substantial risk of HIV infection" is NOT NULL). Note that this data element should only be used to include people for service provision and not exclude people.

* Activity ID: HIV.B6 Capture or update client history
  * Data Element ID: HIV.PRV.DE11
  * Data Element Label: HIV status of contact
  * Description and Definition: The HIV status of the client's contact
  * Multiple Choice Type (if applicable): Select one
  * Data Type: Coding
  * Input Options: N/A
  * Quantity Sub-type: N/A
  * Calculation: N/A
  * Validation Condition: None
  * Required: O
  * Explain Conditionality: None
  * Linkages to Decision Support Tables: 
  * Linkages to Aggregate Indicators: HTS.5
  * Annotations: Contacts defined as current or past sexual partner(s), biological children/parents (if index case is a child) or anyone with whom a needle was shared. Biological children should only include children of an HIV-positive mother. Children of male-index clients (fathers) should only be included when the biological mother is HIV-positive, she is deceased or her HIV status is not known or not documented. Conversely, if the index client is the child, his/her mother should be tested, and if the mother is HIV-positive or deceased, the father should be tested as well. In addition, all biologic siblings of the index child should be tested

* Activity ID: HIV.D8 Capture or update client history
  * Data Element ID: 
  * Data Element Label: HIV clinical stage
  * Description and Definition: WHO clinical stage of client based on signs and symptoms. WHO clinical staging is a way to categorize HIV disease severity based on new or recurrent clinical events. There are 4 WHO clinical stages that range from mild symptoms (WHO clinical stage 1) to severe symptoms (WHO clinical stage 4).
